req.title: System-Admin creates onboarding-task
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to create a onboarding-task through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers UI form creation of an onboarding-task by a system-admin. Test design covers: (1) EP positive path – valid payload yields 200/201 and audit record; (2) NEG path – missing mandatory field yields 400; (3) BVA – task title at exact max-length boundary is accepted; (4) DT – unauthenticated request yields 401; (5) NEG – forbidden role (non-admin) yields 403. Test[4] previously used heuristic=STC which is inappropriate for a negative kind; corrected to NEG since this is a negative equivalence partition (authenticated user with insufficient role). Together these cover all stated expected outcomes and error pathways.

tests.name: test_create_onboarding_task_valid_payload
actions: Build a valid payload with all mandatory fields populated
POST payload to /onboarding-tasks with admin auth header
Capture response status code and body
Query audit log for the returned entity id
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
Audit record exists for the created entity id
trace: R-WEB-025 – audit record written on success
R-WEB-025 – response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
def test_create_onboarding_task_valid_payload(admin_session, audit_log_client):
    payload = {
        "title": "Complete HR induction",
        "description": "New hire must complete all induction steps",
        "due_days": 5,
        "assignee_role": "employee"
    }
    resp = admin_session.post("/onboarding-tasks", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"
    audit_entry = audit_log_client.find(entity_id=entity_id, action="CREATE")
    assert audit_entry is not None, "Audit record must exist for created task"
    admin_session.delete(f"/onboarding-tasks/{entity_id}")

tests.name: test_create_onboarding_task_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
actions: Build a payload with the mandatory 'title' field omitted
POST payload to /onboarding-tasks with admin auth header
Capture response status code and validation error body
expected: Response body contains validation error referencing 'title'
trace: R-WEB-025 – error pathway: 400 on validation error
R-WEB-025 – mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
def test_create_onboarding_task_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(admin_session):
    payload = {
        "description": "Missing title field",
        "due_days": 3,
        "assignee_role": "employee"
    }
    resp = admin_session.post("/onboarding-tasks", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "title" in error_text, "Validation error must reference the missing 'title' field"

tests.name: test_create_onboarding_task_title_at_max_length_boundary
preconditions: Maximum allowed title length is 255 characters
actions: Build a payload with title exactly 255 characters long
POST payload to /onboarding-tasks with admin auth header
Capture response status code and entity id
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
trace: R-WEB-025 – boundary: title field at maximum permitted length
R-WEB-025 – response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
def test_create_onboarding_task_title_at_max_length_boundary(admin_session):
    max_title = "T" * 255
    payload = {
        "title": max_title,
        "description": "Boundary title length test",
        "due_days": 1,
        "assignee_role": "employee"
    }
    resp = admin_session.post("/onboarding-tasks", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201 at max boundary, got {resp.status_code}"
    entity_id = resp.json().get("id")
    assert entity_id, "Entity id must be present for max-length title"
    admin_session.delete(f"/onboarding-tasks/{entity_id}")

tests.name: test_create_onboarding_task_unauthenticated_returns_401
actions: POST payload to /onboarding-tasks without any auth header

tests.name: test_create_onboarding_task_forbidden_role_returns_403
actions: POST payload to /onboarding-tasks using employee-role session
trace: R-WEB-025 – error pathway: 403 if role forbidden
R-WEB-025 – negative partition: authenticated user with insufficient role is rejected
